description.p: Most screenshot tools hand you a rectangle of pixels and walk away. Unisic covers the whole workflow that starts the moment you press the hotkey: annotate directly on the selection overlay before the shot is even taken, keep editing afterwards, record the same region as a GIF or video, and push the result wherever it belongs — clipboard, disk, or a custom upload destination with the link ready to paste.

ul.li: Capture full screen (multi-monitor), an interactive region, or a specific window
Annotate on the overlay before the capture is finalized — ShareX-style
Post-capture editor: arrows, shapes, freehand, text, blur/pixelate, crop, numbered steps, highlight, object cutout with background removal, undo/redo
Record a region or the full screen to GIF, MP4 or WebM, with optional system and microphone audio
Modular upload destinations (custom HTTP/FTP/SFTP/API), auto-copy the link, upload history
Tray icon, configurable global hotkeys, and optional OCR to copy text out of a capture

description.p: Built for Wayland on legitimate APIs only (xdg-desktop-portal, KWin ScreenShot2, PipeWire, KGlobalAccel). KDE Plasma gets the fully silent native capture path; other desktops work through portals. Zero telemetry.

screenshot.caption: Pick full screen, region or window and what happens after the shot
Annotate with arrows, numbered steps, blur and crop, then copy, save or upload
Record the screen, a region or a window to MP4, WebM or GIF
Modular upload destinations with the link copied after every upload
Searchable settings with import and export

description.p: Fixes global hotkeys on GNOME: shortcut registration now carries the app identity on a dedicated D-Bus connection, so binds no longer fail for terminal and AppImage launches. The version footer shows the exact git-commit date of the build, the hotkeys-unavailable card explains the GNOME custom-shortcuts route, and 42 missing translations were filled in.

Fully automatic updates: the app checks GitHub daily, AppImage installs download and swap themselves in place (restarting when idle), and deb/rpm downloads register the signed OBS/COPR repository on install so every later version arrives natively through the package manager. New Updates section in Settings.

Fixes region recording on multi-monitor setups. Adds a dedicated copy-last-capture shortcut (replacing the 2-second Ctrl+C grab), Ctrl+C on the selection overlay, capture-on-release, a separate recordings folder and a trash sound.
Editable shapes and shape groups in the editor, OCR text selection, richer text styling, Spanish and Italian translations, and a separate sound cue when a recording or GIF finishes encoding.
New app ID app.unisic.Unisic (backed by unisic.app). Stale menu and autostart entries from older installs are cleaned up automatically.
